message:
🌱 jaeger: 1.16 docker: query's JAEGER_AGENT_PORT (#1152)

@@ -0,0 +1,72 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "jaeger-1986-1-16-docker-query-s-jaeger-agent-port",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "jaeger: 1.16 docker: query's JAEGER_AGENT_PORT",
+    "description": "1.16 docker: query's JAEGER_AGENT_PORT. Requested by 7+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current jaeger deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your jaeger version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n observability -l app.kubernetes.io/name=jaeger\nhelm list -n observability 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working jaeger installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review jaeger configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant jaeger configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n observability -l app.kubernetes.io/name=jaeger\nkubectl get configmap -n observability -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=jaeger\n```\n## Requirement - what kind of business use case are you trying to solve?\n\nQuery outputs:\n\"error\":\"cannot obtain reporter config from env: cannot parse env var JAEGER_AGENT_PORT=udp://10.90.32.41:5775\n\n## Problem - what in Jaeger blocks you from"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for 1.16 docker: query's JAEGER_AGENT_PORT",
+        "description": "> Note that this happens only when the Jaeger instance is called `jaeger`, which is arguably a very common name for the Jaeger instance:\n> \n> * [grafana/loki#480](https://github.com/grafana/loki/issues/480)\n> * [observatorium/configuration#127](https://github.com/observatorium/configuration/pull/127)\n> \n> One possible solution is to ignore the env var value if the value is not an integer, without breaking the agent because of it.\n> \n> Question: do we want to address this in the Jaeger Go\n\nSee the source issue for community-verified solutions."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n observability -l app.kubernetes.io/name=jaeger\nkubectl get events -n observability --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"1.16 docker: query's JAEGER_AGENT_PORT\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "> Note that this happens only when the Jaeger instance is called `jaeger`, which is arguably a very common name for the Jaeger instance:\n> \n> * [grafana/loki#480](https://github.com/grafana/loki/issues/480)\n> * [observatorium/configuration#127](https://github.com/observatorium/configuration/pull/127)\n> \n> One possible solution is to ignore the env var value if the value is not an integer, without",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
